events.text: Patient is a 47-year-old Caucasian male who presented with a two-week history of worsening fatigue, easy bruising, and subjective fevers.
At age 47, the patient was diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia NOS, ICD-O morphology code 9861/3, primary site bone marrow C42.1.

At age 47, initial complete blood count showed white blood cell count of 6,800/uL with 38% blasts, hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, and platelet count of 48,000/uL. LDH was 620 U/L. Coagulation studies including fibrinogen and D-dimer were within normal limits.

At age 47, bone marrow biopsy with aspirate showed a hypercellular marrow (85% cellularity) with 52% myeloblasts. Blasts were medium-sized with scant cytoplasm and dispersed chromatin. Flow cytometry demonstrated blasts positive for CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, HLA-DR, MPO, and with aberrant CD56 expression.

At age 47, next-generation sequencing identified FLT3-ITD mutation with allelic ratio 0.61, NPM1 mutation (type B insertion), DNMT3A splice site mutation, and TET2 loss-of-function mutation. No TP53, RUNX1, or ASXL1 mutations detected.

At age 47, conventional cytogenetics revealed a normal male karyotype, 46,XY, in all 20 metaphases analyzed. ELN 2022 risk was classified as intermediate based on co-mutated FLT3-ITD and NPM1 in the setting of a normal karyotype.

At age 47, the patient began induction with 7+3 regimen of cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion for seven days and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily from days eight through twenty-one.

At age 47, the patient developed prolonged febrile neutropenia lasting 22 days during induction. CT imaging showed bilateral lower lobe consolidations and bronchoalveolar lavage cultures grew Stenotrophomonas maltophilia, treated with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole.
At age 47, grade 2 mucositis and grade 2 diarrhea occurred during induction, managed with supportive care including magic mouthwash and loperamide.
At age 47, day 14 bone marrow biopsy showed a hypocellular marrow (8% cellularity) with 2% residual blasts by morphology, consistent with a good early response.

At age 47, day 28 bone marrow biopsy confirmed complete remission with 1.4% blasts. Flow cytometry MRD was positive at 0.08%. NPM1 transcript by RT-qPCR showed a 2.9 log reduction from baseline, not yet achieving molecular remission.

At age 47, consolidation cycle one with high-dose cytarabine 3 g/m2 every twelve hours on days one, three, and five plus midostaurin was initiated. Given persistent MRD positivity and DNMT3A co-mutation as an adverse prognostic factor, allogeneic transplant evaluation was pursued simultaneously.
At age 47, during consolidation the patient developed Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea with 10 watery stools per day, treated with oral fidaxomicin.

At age 47, after two cycles of consolidation with continued MRD positivity by flow cytometry, the patient proceeded to allogeneic stem cell transplant from a ten-of-ten HLA-matched unrelated donor with myeloablative busulfan and fludarabine conditioning.
At age 47, GVHD prophylaxis was tacrolimus and methotrexate. Neutrophil engraftment occurred on day plus 13 and platelet engraftment on day plus 17.

At age 47, the patient developed grade 2 acute GVHD of the skin and liver with bilirubin rising to 4.2 mg/dL and diffuse maculopapular rash over 60% body surface area, requiring systemic prednisone 1 mg/kg.

At age 47, GVHD responded to corticosteroids and the patient was slowly tapered over three months. Day plus 100 bone marrow demonstrated complete donor chimerism at 99.5% with no morphologic disease and negative MRD by both flow cytometry and NPM1 RT-qPCR.

At age 48, at one year post-transplant the patient developed chronic GVHD with sicca symptoms, mild skin sclerosis, and oral lichenoid changes, managed with low-dose prednisone and ruxolitinib. He remained in complete molecular remission.
At age 48, PET-CT at one year post-transplant showed no FDG-avid disease. Mild thymic rebound hyperplasia was noted, considered a normal finding post-transplant.

scenario_blurb: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with FLT3-ITD mutation. ELN intermediate/adverse risk. Induction with 7+3 plus midostaurin. Some achieving CR, some refractory. Include cytogenetics (normal karyotype or complex), blast percentage monitoring, allogeneic stem cell transplant in first remission for eligible patients.
